# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for container security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Python runtime as a parent image 
FROM python:3-slim   # or any other base docker images you prefer, e.g., nodejs for NodeJS applications etc...   
    
# Set the working directory in the container to /app and set environment variables (replace with your app's name)     
WORKDIR /app 
      
# Copy local code into image  
COPY . ./  # assuming all files are under current folder. Replace as per requirement   
    
# Install production dependencies using requirements file in the project directory       
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt     
        
EXPOSE 80, 5432, 6379   # Expose ports for services that will be used by your application   
    
# Run app on container startup (replace with command to run the service)       
CMD ["python", "app.py"]
